page: Microsoft Azure D160alds_v7 is a virtual machine instance from the Daldsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD processor with 160 dedicated vCPUs. The system is configured with 320.0 GB of system memory, which establishes a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This instance includes 9449 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-throughput disk configurations. It does not include any graphics processing units and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The D160alds_v7 is positioned as a low-memory, compute-heavy instance. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it a cost-efficient option for workloads that require massive parallel processing power without the high memory overhead costs associated with standard or memory-optimized instances. Tradeoffs include the low memory allocation per core, which limits its suitability for memory-intensive in-memory databases. Appropriate workloads include batch processing, large-scale web serving, compute-intensive tasks, and applications utilizing the substantial local storage capacity.
description: Microsoft Azure D160alds_v7 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine from the Daldsv7 family, powered by an AMD processor and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 160 dedicated vCPUs and 320.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low-memory ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. The instance is equipped with 9449 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, though it lacks GPU acceleration and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy operations that do not require large memory footprints, avoiding the premium pricing of memory-dense configurations. The D160alds_v7 is engineered for workloads such as large-scale batch processing, compute-intensive applications, and storage-heavy deployments requiring high parallel processing capacity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D160alds_v7 is a Daldsv7-family virtual machine featuring 160 dedicated AMD vCPUs, 320.0 GB of RAM, and 9449 GB of local storage, optimized for compute-heavy and low-memory workloads.

tagline: A high-core, low-memory virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD processors and substantial local storage for parallel compute workloads.
